title: Meghalaya Waterfalls & Root Bridges
shortDescription: Five days through the wettest place on earth — living root bridges, glowing Dawki river, and the cleanest village in Asia.
highlights: Trek to Nongriat double-decker living root bridge
Boat ride on the crystal-clear Dawki river
Sunrise at Umiam Lake and Elephant Falls
Walk through Mawlynnong, Asia's cleanest village

itinerary.title: Arrival in Shillong
itinerary.description: Arrive at Shillong, check in, and head to Umiam Lake for a serene lakeside evening with views of the surrounding hills.

itinerary.title: Elephant Falls & Cherrapunji
itinerary.description: Visit the three-tiered Elephant Falls in the morning, then drive to Cherrapunji — one of the wettest places on earth — and explore the Nohkalikai waterfall viewpoint.

itinerary.title: Nongriat Root Bridge Trek
itinerary.description: Descend 3,500 steps into the valley at Nongriat to reach the iconic double-decker living root bridge, grown over 500 years from Ficus elastica roots by the Khasi people.

itinerary.title: Dawki & Mawlynnong
itinerary.description: Take a coracle boat on the glass-clear Dawki river along the Bangladesh border, then walk the spotless bamboo-paved lanes of Mawlynnong village.

itinerary.title: Local Market & Departure
itinerary.description: Morning browse at Shillong's Bara Bazaar for local produce and handwoven textiles, then depart with a farewell meal.

included: Private AC vehicle for all transfers & sightseeing
All monument and waterfall entry fees

title: Kaziranga Rhino Safari
shortDescription: Jeep and elephant-back safaris in the UNESCO-listed grasslands that shelter two-thirds of the world's one-horned rhinos.
highlights: Elephant-back safari at dawn through tall elephant grass
Jeep safari in the Central and Western ranges
Spot Indian one-horned rhino, wild buffalo, and tigers
Visit to the Kaziranga Orchid and Biodiversity Park

itinerary.title: Arrival & Evening Briefing
itinerary.description: Arrive at Kaziranga, check in to the jungle-edge resort, and attend an evening naturalist briefing on wildlife behaviour and safari zones.

itinerary.title: Elephant Safari & Jeep Safari
itinerary.description: Pre-dawn elephant-back safari through the misty Central Range floodplains, followed by a jeep safari in the Western Range with chances to see rhino, wild elephant, and swamp deer.

itinerary.title: Orchid Park & Departure
itinerary.description: Morning visit to the Kaziranga Orchid and Biodiversity Park showcasing over 500 orchid species, then depart after brunch.

title: Tawang Monastery Expedition
shortDescription: A high-altitude journey through Arunachal Pradesh to India's largest monastery, crossing the 13,700 ft Sela Pass and the mythical Madhuri Lake.
highlights: Cross the Sela Pass at 13,700 ft with frozen lake views
Explore Tawang Monastery, the largest in India
Bum La border pass visit (permits arranged)
Reflections at the serene Madhuri Lake

itinerary.title: Guwahati to Bhalukpong
itinerary.description: Fly into Guwahati and drive east into Arunachal Pradesh, crossing the permit checkpoint at Bhalukpong and arriving in the foothills by evening.

itinerary.title: Bhalukpong to Dirang
itinerary.description: Drive through sub-tropical forest to Dirang, visiting the ancient stone Dirang Dzong and the apple orchards that line the Kameng River.

itinerary.title: Sela Pass & Tawang
itinerary.description: Cross the iconic Sela Pass at 13,700 ft beside a frozen alpine lake and descend into Tawang Valley for check-in and acclimatisation.

itinerary.title: Tawang Monastery
itinerary.description: Full morning at the 400-year-old Tawang Monastery — one of the largest Buddhist gompas in Asia — and the Tawang War Memorial honouring the 1962 battle.

itinerary.title: Bum La Pass
itinerary.description: Permitted day excursion to the Bum La border pass on the Indo-China Line of Actual Control at 15,200 ft, surrounded by barren high-altitude terrain.

itinerary.title: Madhuri Lake & Shonga-tser
itinerary.description: Morning at the turquoise Madhuri Lake (Shonga-tser), set against a panorama of snow peaks made famous in Bollywood — one of Arunachal's most photogenic spots.

itinerary.title: Return & Departure
itinerary.description: Begin the long drive back toward Guwahati or Tezpur for onward flights, passing the pine forests of the Eastern Himalayan foothills.

included: Private SUV with experienced mountain driver
Inner Line Permit (ILP) and Bum La permits
All entry fees and monastery visits

title: Gangtok & North Sikkim Explorer
shortDescription: From Gangtok's MG Marg to the flower valleys of Yumthang and the snow desert at Zero Point — Sikkim in full colour.
highlights: Tsomgo Lake and Nathu La pass on the Silk Road
Yumthang Valley Valley of Flowers at 11,800 ft
Zero Point snowfields at 15,100 ft
Evening stroll on Gangtok's car-free MG Marg

itinerary.title: Arrival in Gangtok
itinerary.description: Check in to your hotel in Gangtok and spend the evening on MG Marg — Sikkim's pedestrian promenade lined with cafés, craft shops, and mountain views.

itinerary.title: Tsomgo Lake & Nathu La
itinerary.description: Drive up to the glacial Tsomgo Lake at 12,400 ft and then to Nathu La, the ancient Silk Road mountain pass on the India-China border at 14,140 ft.

itinerary.title: Gangtok to Lachung
itinerary.description: Drive north into Lachung Valley, passing the Naga Falls and Seven Sisters Waterfalls cascade along a dramatic gorge road.

itinerary.title: Yumthang Valley
itinerary.description: Morning excursion into Yumthang Valley, a high-altitude meadow filled with rhododendrons, primulas, and hot springs, ringed by Himalayan peaks.

itinerary.title: Zero Point
itinerary.description: Drive to Zero Point at 15,100 ft — the last Indian outpost before the Tibet border — a barren snowfield with sweeping views of glaciated peaks.

itinerary.title: Return to Gangtok & Departure
itinerary.description: Drive back to Gangtok for a farewell lunch, pick up local cardamom and chhurpi cheese at the market, and transfer for departure.

included: Private vehicle for all transfers and excursions
Protected Area Permit (PAP) for North Sikkim
excluded: Flights to Bagdogra or train to NJP

title: Hornbill Festival Nagaland
shortDescription: Experience all 17 Naga tribes under one roof at Kisama Heritage Village during India's most vibrant tribal festival.
highlights: Kisama Heritage Village — morungs of all 17 Naga tribes
Traditional Naga cuisine tasting and cooking demo
Kohima War Cemetery — one of WW2's most moving memorials
Khonoma green village, Asia's first conservation village

itinerary.title: Arrival in Kohima
itinerary.description: Arrive in Kohima, settle in, and visit the Kohima War Cemetery where Allied soldiers from the decisive 1944 battle rest among the hills.

itinerary.title: Hornbill Festival — Kisama
itinerary.description: Full day at the Hornbill Festival grounds in Kisama Heritage Village, watching dance performances, warrior demonstrations, and traditional games across the tribal morungs (community halls).

itinerary.title: Naga Cuisine & Cultural Immersion
itinerary.description: Morning Naga food trail — smoked pork, Naga chilli chutneys, and fermented bamboo shoot dishes — followed by a weaving and craft demonstration with local artisans.

itinerary.title: Khonoma Village & Departure
itinerary.description: Visit Khonoma, the 600-year-old fortified village famed as Asia's first green village with community-managed forests, then depart after lunch.

title: Majuli River Island Retreat
shortDescription: Slow travel on the world's largest river island — Vaishnavite satras, Mising stilt houses, and sunrise cycling along the Brahmaputra.
highlights: Ferry crossing on the Brahmaputra to reach the island
Samaguri Satra — live mask-making by resident monks
Overnight stay in a Mising community stilt house
Sunrise bicycle ride along the river embankment

itinerary.title: Jorhat to Majuli by Ferry
itinerary.description: Drive to Neamati Ghat and take the public ferry across the mighty Brahmaputra — a 1-hour crossing through braided river channels and sandbanks — then cycle to your eco-stay.

itinerary.title: Satras & Mising Village
itinerary.description: Visit the 500-year-old Auniati and Kamalabari Vaishnavite satras (monasteries), watch monks create the island's famous papier-mâché masks at Samaguri Satra, and spend the night in a Mising stilt house above the floodplain.

itinerary.title: Sunrise Ride & Departure
itinerary.description: Early morning cycling along the embankment roads as egrets and storks lift off the wetlands, then return ferry and departure from Jorhat.

included: 2 nights accommodation (1 homestay + 1 eco-lodge) with breakfast
Local guide and satra entry donations

title: Ziro Valley Culture Trail
shortDescription: Walk through the pine-clad UNESCO-nominated Ziro Valley, sleep in Apatani villages, and learn the unique paddy-cum-fish farming system.
highlights: Village walks in Hong and Hija Apatani settlements
Paddy-cum-fish integrated field system — unique to the Apatani
Talley Valley Wildlife Sanctuary forest walk
Home-cooked meal with an Apatani family

itinerary.title: Arrival in Ziro
itinerary.description: Fly into Itanagar and drive to Ziro, arriving in the valley famous for its cool climate, pine forests, and the Apatani tribal homeland.

itinerary.title: Hong & Hija Village Walk
itinerary.description: Walk through the traditional Apatani villages of Hong and Hija, observing the intricately planned paddy-cum-fish fields where rice and fish are cultivated together in the same terraced plots.

itinerary.title: Talley Valley
itinerary.description: Day hike into Talley Valley Wildlife Sanctuary, a pristine subtropical broadleaf forest with orchids, hornbills, and the Talley River cascading through undisturbed jungle.

itinerary.title: Local Kitchen & Departure
itinerary.description: Morning in a local Apatani kitchen learning to prepare pika pila (preserved mustard leaf) and apong (rice beer), then depart for Itanagar or Naharlagun.

included: Talley Valley forest trek with naturalist

title: Dzukou Valley Trek
shortDescription: Trek into the 'valley of flowers of the North East' — a remote highland meadow famous for Dzukou lilies, stream crossings, and pristine caves.
highlights: Ascent from Viswema trailhead through rhododendron forest
Overnight camp in the heart of Dzukou Valley
Dzukou lily bloom (June–August) carpeting the valley
Cave exploration and stream crossings along the ridge

itinerary.title: Kohima to Viswema
itinerary.description: Drive to Viswema village — the southern trailhead for Dzukou — meet your trek guide and porters, and acclimatise with a short evening walk.

itinerary.title: Ascent to Dzukou Valley
itinerary.description: Trek 8 km through bamboo groves and rhododendron forest, gaining 1,100 ft to the valley floor at 7,800 ft; set up camp beside the Dzukou stream.

itinerary.title: Valley Exploration
itinerary.description: Full day exploring the valley ridgeline — Dzukou lily meadows, natural limestone caves, and the sweeping views toward the Japfü Peak range.

itinerary.title: Descent & Departure
itinerary.description: Early descent back to Viswema, drive to Kohima for a celebratory Naga meal, then depart.

title: Loktak Lake & Imphal Discovery
shortDescription: Explore Imphal's warrior heritage and the floating phumdis of Loktak — home to the world's only floating national park and the endangered sangai deer.
highlights: Boat ride over the phumdis (floating biomass islands) of Loktak
Keibul Lamjao National Park — the world's only floating national park
Ima Keithel, the all-women market in Imphal
Kangla Fort — the ancient seat of Manipuri kings

itinerary.title: Arrival in Imphal
itinerary.description: Arrive at Imphal airport, check in, and spend the afternoon at Kangla Fort — the historic citadel of the Meitei kingdom overlooking the Imphal River.

itinerary.title: Ima Keithel & Cultural Imphal
itinerary.description: Morning at Ima Keithel (Mothers' Market), run entirely by women for 500 years, followed by the Manipur State Museum and a Ras Lila dance performance in the evening.

itinerary.title: Loktak Lake & Keibul Lamjao
itinerary.description: Drive to Loktak Lake and take a boat across the phumdis — floating mats of vegetation that support entire communities — into Keibul Lamjao National Park in search of the sangai (brow-antlered deer).

itinerary.title: Sendra Island & Departure
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sendra Island hilltop for panoramic lake views, then transfer to Imphal airport for departure.

included: Loktak boat ride and park entry fee

title: Namdapha Wilderness Trail
shortDescription: Venture into India's easternmost wilderness — a biodiversity hotspot in Arunachal Pradesh where four big-cat species and hoolock gibbons share the rainforest.
highlights: Rainforest walks from Deban forest camp
Noa-Dehing river crossing and riverside camp
Spot hornbills, hoolock gibbons, and rare Namdapha flying squirrel
Drive through Miao — gateway to one of India's least-visited parks

itinerary.title: Dibrugarh to Miao
itinerary.description: Fly to Dibrugarh and drive into Arunachal Pradesh to Miao, the small market town at the edge of Namdapha Tiger Reserve, arriving by evening.

itinerary.title: Miao to Deban
itinerary.description: Drive the forest road into the park core, arriving at Deban forest camp — the main base inside Namdapha — set beside the Noa-Dehing river with views of the Patkai range.

itinerary.title: Rainforest Trails
itinerary.description: Full day of guided walks on the Deban forest trails, listening for the whooping calls of hoolock gibbons overhead and scanning the canopy for great Indian hornbills and Blyth's kingfishers.

itinerary.title: Noa-Dehing River Walk
itinerary.description: Follow the Noa-Dehing river upstream through bamboo forest and rocky stream crossings — one of eastern India's most pristine river corridors — with chances of elephant and gaur sightings.

itinerary.title: Return to Miao & Departure
itinerary.description: Early morning bird walk around Deban, then drive back to Miao and onward to Dibrugarh for departure.

included: All meals during the trek and camp stay
Expert naturalist guide and forest entry permits
excluded: Inner Line Permit for Arunachal Pradesh
